$grounding: Grounded in: docs/design/ui-dual-tone-tokens.json (admin tone tokens), apps/admin/app/lib/ui.tsx (AppShell/icons wrappers, tone='admin'), apps/admin/app/lib/branding.ts (per-tenant accent flows via --lms-accent; never hard-coded), apps/admin/app/courses/page.tsx + courses/[id]/edit/page.tsx (existing list + form + non-admin gating + .lms-table-wrap region pattern), handshake feat-content-author-rich-pages.md §4 D5 (7 routes) + D6 (admin authoring screen scope). All tokens are REUSED from the admin tone; NO new palette invented. Net-new pattern (RichTextEditor + accessible toolbar) is flagged in openQuestions — everything else reuses existing @lms/ui components.

screens.goal: A teacher reviews the rich pages they have authored for a course, sees each page's draft/published status at a glance, and starts a new page or opens one to edit.
informationHierarchy: Page title (links to editor) — what the page is
Status (draft vs published) — is it live to learners
Last updated timestamp + version count — freshness / activity
Course context (which course these pages belong to) in PageHeader subtitle

screens.goal: A teacher writes and formats rich content with a keyboard-operable toolbar, embeds media/files via signed upload, saves as a draft, and publishes — with a version history they can inspect.
informationHierarchy: Status indicator (Draft / Published / Unsaved changes) — the single most important orientation cue
The editing canvas (rich-text body) — where the work happens
Title + slug fields (identity of the page)
Primary actions: Save draft / Publish + their save status (saving / saved / error)
Version history (secondary, in an aside on desktop / collapsible panel on phone)
screens.wiresTo: POST /courses/:courseId/pages (1), GET /pages/:id (3), PATCH /pages/:id (4 — body ⇒ new draft version), POST /pages/:id/publish (5), GET /pages/:id/versions (6), GET /pages/:id/versions/:versionId (7), POST /uploads (existing signed-upload for media embed, architect D4)

designTokens.$note: All values are the existing ADMIN tone from docs/design/ui-dual-tone-tokens.json, exposed at runtime as --lms-* CSS vars. accent is the TENANT accent via --lms-accent (fallback admin #0E5FAE / admin branding.ts #6a8cff) — never hard-code. Reference vars, not literals, in the build.

color.accentSoft: var(--lms-accent-soft) /* active toolbar button + active nav fill */

typography.fontFamily: var(--lms-font-family) /* tenant font, else system-ui fallback */
typography.editorBody: 16px / 1.5; headings inside the canvas follow the h1->h4 ramp from the token spec so authored content matches rendered learner content

shadow.lg: var(--lms-shadow-lg) /* used for the slug/help nothing-heavy; sticky toolbar uses sm */

regions: aside (version history — desktop only; collapses into main flow on tablet/phone)

editorComposition.desktop: Two-column: main editor column (minmax(0,1fr)) + version-history aside (~320px fixed) via CSS grid; aside is a sibling <aside> landmark, sticky-top below the toolbar.
editorComposition.tablet: Single column; version history becomes a full-width collapsible <details>/disclosure Card below the editor actions.
editorComposition.phone: Single column, stacked; toolbar wraps to multiple rows but stays sticky; version history is a collapsible disclosure at the bottom. No element exceeds viewport width at 360px.

breakpoints.approach: mobile-first, fluid between breakpoints; min-width:0 + overflow-wrap:anywhere on text cells; the page list uses the existing .lms-table-wrap role=region scroll container so columns are never silently clipped; editor canvas and toolbar never overflow horizontally at 360px (toolbar wraps, canvas is fluid width)

components.name: PageHeader (reused @lms/ui)
components.purpose: Title + subtitle + actions slot at top of both screens; consistent with courses screens.
anatomy: h1 title
subtitle (course context)
actions slot (status Chip on editor / New page Button on list)
components.content: List: title 'Pages' subtitle 'Author rich content pages for {Course title}. Drafts stay private until you publish.' · Editor: title 'New page' or 'Edit page' subtitle the page title; actions = status Chip.

components.name: PagesTable (reuse .lms-table + .lms-table-wrap region pattern from courses/page.tsx)
components.purpose: List the course's pages, scannable, with status and actions; data-dense admin tone.
anatomy: div.lms-table-wrap role=region aria-label='Course pages' tabIndex=0
table.lms-table
thead th scope=col: Page | Status | Updated | Actions
tbody rows: title (link, color var(--lms-accent), weight 600) + slug muted meta; status Chip; relative/absolute updated time; action Buttons (Edit secondary sm; Publish primary sm if draft)
components.content: Row example — title 'Course syllabus and grading policy', slug '/course-syllabus-and-grading-policy', status 'Draft', updated 'Updated 2 hours ago', actions Edit / Publish.

responsive.phone: first column min-width 200px inside the horizontal-scroll region; never silent overflow
responsive.tablet: condensed; may hide the slug meta line

components.interactions: Title is an <a> to the editor; action Buttons are real buttons/links; whole row not clickable (SR clarity per token Table a11y note).

a11y.role: table in a labelled scrollable region
a11y.label: aria-label='Course pages'; th scope=col
a11y.keyboard: Tab through links/buttons; region is focusable so keyboard users can scroll it

components.name: StatusIndicator (reuse Chip @lms/ui)
components.purpose: Show the page's lifecycle state without relying on color alone (text + tone).
anatomy: Chip with text label
components.content: 'Draft' (tone warning), 'Published' (tone success), 'Unsaved changes' (tone neutral/warning) in editor header.

responsive.phone: sits under title in PageHeader actions

a11y.label: meaning carried by the text, not color (WCAG noColorOnly)

components.name: TitleSlugFields (reuse Field + Input @lms/ui)
components.purpose: Set page identity; slug auto-derives from title (architect D5 route 1) but is editable.
anatomy: Field label 'Title' required → Input
Field label 'URL slug' → Input with leading '/' prefix affordance + help text 'Auto-generated from the title. Letters, numbers and hyphens only.'
components.content: Title placeholder none (label-driven); slug shows derived value e.g. 'course-syllabus-and-grading-policy'.
variants: create (slug live-derives as you type until manually edited)
states: invalid (border var(--lms-danger), aria-invalid, error text via aria-describedby — e.g. 'A page with this slug already exists in this course.')
components.interactions: Title input drives slug until the user edits slug manually; both validate on blur and on submit.

a11y.label: explicit <label> per Field; slug error referenced by aria-describedby; aria-invalid on error

components.name: RichTextEditor (NET-NEW — flagged in openQuestions)
components.purpose: Accessible WYSIWYG editing surface producing sanitized HTML stored as page_version.body (architect D3). frontend-dev picks the library; this spec fixes the a11y + visual contract.
anatomy: FormattingToolbar (sticky, role=toolbar) — see separate component

EditingCanvas — role=textbox aria-multiline=true contenteditable region, bg var(--lms-surface), 1px border var(--lms-border-strong), radius var(--lms-radius-sm), padding var(--lms-space-4), min-height ~360px, body 16px/1.5
MediaEmbed dialog/menu (triggered from toolbar 'Insert media' button)
components.content: Empty canvas placeholder (subtle, via aria-label not a fake value): 'Start writing your page. Use the toolbar to format text, add headings, lists, links, and media.' Real authored example: an <h2> 'Week 1 — Getting started', a paragraph, a bulleted list, and an embedded image.
variants: read-only (when viewing a historical version — canvas aria-readonly=true, toolbar hidden/disabled)
states: focus-visible (3px var(--lms-focus) ring on the canvas, offset 2px)
empty (placeholder visible, accessible via aria-describedby help text — not placeholder text masquerading as content)
loading (skeleton .lms-skeleton block where canvas will be)
upload-in-progress (inline progress note 'Uploading image…' near insertion point; non-blocking)
upload-error (inline Alert tone=danger 'Upload failed. Try again.')

responsive.phone: canvas full-width, min-height shorter (~240px); toolbar wraps above it and stays sticky
responsive.desktop: canvas fills main column; aside history beside it

components.interactions: Standard text editing; keyboard shortcuts mirror toolbar (Ctrl/Cmd+B bold, Ctrl/Cmd+I italic, Ctrl/Cmd+K link). Pasting strips disallowed HTML (sanitize on input — security-agent gate per D3).

a11y.role: textbox (aria-multiline=true) with an accessible name 'Page content' and aria-describedby pointing at the format help + status
a11y.label: labelled 'Page content'; toolbar is associated as the region's controls
a11y.keyboard: Editor is reachable by Tab; toolbar uses roving tabindex (see toolbar); Escape from a toolbar menu/dialog returns focus to the canvas at the prior caret position

components.name: FormattingToolbar (NET-NEW — accessible toolbar; flagged in openQuestions)
components.purpose: Keyboard-operable formatting controls for the editor (WCAG 2.2 AA — AC #1).
anatomy: div role=toolbar aria-label='Text formatting' aria-controls=<canvas id>
Grouped buttons: [Bold, Italic, Underline] | [H2, H3, Paragraph] | [Bulleted list, Numbered list] | [Link, Insert media/file] | [Undo, Redo]
Each button: 36px (--sm) icon button with min 44x44 hit area via padding, inline-SVG icon (aria-hidden) + visually-hidden text label, aria-pressed for toggle states
sticky: position sticky, top 0 within scroll context, bg var(--lms-surface-2), border-bottom 1px var(--lms-border-strong), z-index 150
components.content: Tooltip/label text: 'Bold', 'Italic', 'Underline', 'Heading 2', 'Heading 3', 'Paragraph', 'Bulleted list', 'Numbered list', 'Insert link', 'Insert media or file', 'Undo', 'Redo'.
variants: compact (phone: groups wrap, separators become row breaks)
states: button-disabled (e.g. Undo with no history — aria-disabled, opacity .5)

responsive.phone: wraps to 2-3 rows, full toolbar width; sticky

components.interactions: Roving tabindex: ONE tab stop for the whole toolbar; Left/Right Arrow move between buttons, Home/End jump to first/last, Enter/Space activate, Escape moves focus back to the canvas. Toggle buttons reflect caret state via aria-pressed.

a11y.label: aria-label='Text formatting'; each control has a text name (visually-hidden span or aria-label); toggles use aria-pressed; group separators are decorative (aria-hidden)
a11y.keyboard: WAI-ARIA Toolbar pattern — single tab stop + arrow-key navigation; never a tab-trap; Escape returns to canvas

components.name: MediaEmbedControl (reuses existing signed-upload flow — architect D4)
components.purpose: Embed an image, video, or file by uploading via POST /uploads and inserting the returned blobUrl inline in the HTML (/
anatomy: Toolbar 'Insert media or file' button → opens a small dialog/menu (role=dialog, focus-trapped, Escape closes)
File picker (<input type=file> with accessible label) + accepted-types help
On image: required Field 'Alt text (describe the image)' — enforce non-empty for to keep authored content accessible
Progress + result: 'Uploading…' → inserts node at caret; on file (non-image) inserts a labelled <a> with the filename
components.content: Dialog title 'Insert media or file'; help 'Images, video, PDFs and documents. Max size per your plan.'; alt-text help 'Required for images so screen-reader users understand them.'
states: success (node inserted, focus returns to canvas)
error (Alert tone=danger 'Upload failed. Check the file and try again.', retry)

responsive.phone: dialog is full-width sheet-style, content scrolls, never overflows

components.interactions: Open via toolbar button or Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+M; focus moves into dialog; Escape cancels and returns focus to canvas; on success the inserted media is selected for easy follow-up.

a11y.label: labelled controls; image alt-text field is required and validated
a11y.keyboard: focus trapped while open, Escape closes, focus restored to invoking caret

components.name: EditorActionBar (reuse Button @lms/ui + asg-actionbar layout)
components.purpose: Save draft / Publish, with save status feedback.
anatomy: Right-aligned action row (border-top, like asg-actionbar): [Cancel ghost] [Save draft secondary] [Publish primary]
Inline save-status text/region (aria-live=polite) left of the buttons: 'Saving…' → 'Saved · just now' → on failure an Alert tone=danger
components.content: Buttons 'Cancel', 'Save draft', 'Publish'. Status: 'All changes saved', 'Saving…', 'Couldn't save — retry'. Publish confirm copy (if confirm step kept): 'Publishing makes this page visible to learners in the course.'
variants: create (Publish disabled until first save creates the page)
edit-published (Save draft creates a new draft version; Publish promotes it)
states: saving (Save/Publish buttons show busy + aria-busy, disabled to prevent double-submit)
saved (status region announces 'All changes saved', timestamp)
published (status Chip flips to Published; success Alert 'Page published.')

responsive.phone: buttons stretch full-width (asg-actionbar stretch rule); status sits above them

components.interactions: Save draft → PATCH /pages/:id (body ⇒ new draft version) or POST create on first save. Publish → POST /pages/:id/publish (latest draft). Disable buttons during in-flight requests; never silent failure.

a11y.role: group of buttons + aria-live=polite status region
a11y.label: status changes announced politely; errors via role=alert
a11y.keyboard: buttons in logical tab order after the editor; Enter/Space activate

components.name: VersionHistoryPanel (reuse Card + Stack + Chip; list semantics)
components.purpose: Show all versions newest-first and let the teacher view a historical version (architect D5 routes 6 + 7).
anatomy: aside aria-labelledby='version-history-heading' (desktop) / <details> disclosure (tablet/phone)
h2 'Version history'
ordered list (<ol>) newest-first; each item: 'Version {n}' + state Chip (Draft/Published) + author + relative timestamp + a 'View' Button (secondary sm)
Current/published version marked with an accent dot + visually-hidden 'current' text
components.content: Item example: 'Version 3 · Draft · by A. Teacher · 2 hours ago · [View]'. Empty (brand-new unsaved page): 'No versions yet. Save a draft to create version 1.' Loading: 3 .lms-skeleton rows.
variants: read-only-viewing (a version is open in the canvas; banner 'Viewing version 2 (read-only). [Back to draft]')

responsive.phone: collapsible <details> at bottom of page; summary is a 44px button

components.interactions: 'View' loads GET /pages/:id/versions/:versionId into the canvas read-only with a clear banner + 'Back to current draft'. (Restore is an architect-noted follow-up, NOT in #32 scope — see openQuestions.)

a11y.role: complementary (aside) / disclosure (details); the list is an <ol>
a11y.label: aside labelled by its heading; each 'View' button names its version e.g. 'View version 3'
a11y.keyboard: disclosure summary toggles with Enter/Space and reflects aria-expanded; View buttons in tab order

components.name: EmptyState (reuse @lms/ui EmptyState + content icon)
components.purpose: Pages-list empty state with a clear first action.
anatomy: inline-SVG 'content' icon (document) aria-hidden
title
description
primary action Button
components.content: title 'No pages yet', description 'Create your first page to share rich materials with learners in this course.', action 'New page'.

a11y.label: title is a heading; icon aria-hidden

components.name: PermissionDenied / ServiceOffline / NotFound (reuse Alert @lms/ui)
components.purpose: Cover gating + error flows consistent with courses screens.
anatomy: PageHeader 'Not authorized' / 'Page unavailable'
Alert tone warning/danger with real copy
components.content: Non-admin: 'You are signed in as {user}, but your account does not hold a teaching or administrator role, so page authoring is unavailable.' (tone=warning) · Service offline: 'The content service is unavailable. Start it (CONTENT_STORE=memory pnpm dev in services/content) to author pages here.' (tone=warning) · 404: 'This page no longer exists or was moved.' (tone=warning) · Save error: 'Couldn't save your changes. Check your connection and try again.' (tone=danger, role=alert).
components.interactions: save-error offers retry; others are terminal with a back link

a11y.label: meaning in text + inline-SVG icon, never color alone

flows: Happy path (create): /courses/[id]/pages → New page → type title (slug auto-derives) → write in canvas, format via toolbar, embed an image with required alt text → Save draft (POST create → status 'Saved', version 1 appears in history) → continue editing → Save draft (PATCH → new draft version 2) → Publish (POST publish → status Chip 'Published', success Alert).
Happy path (edit published): open a published page → Save draft creates a new draft version (page stays published to learners until re-publish) → Publish promotes the new draft.
Empty (list): no pages → EmptyState with 'New page' action.
Empty (editor history): brand-new unsaved page → history shows 'No versions yet. Save a draft to create version 1.'
Loading: list shows skeleton rows; editor shows skeleton canvas + skeleton history.
Saving / saved: aria-live status announces 'Saving…' then 'All changes saved'; buttons disabled while in-flight.
Save error: Alert tone=danger role=alert with retry; buttons re-enabled; no data loss (canvas content retained).
Upload (media embed): Insert media → pick file → (image) enter alt text → uploading progress → inserted inline; on failure inline Alert + retry.
Version viewing: View version N → canvas loads read-only with banner + 'Back to current draft'.
Permission denied (non-admin): PageHeader 'Not authorized' + warning Alert; no page data fetched/leaked.
Not found: GET /pages/:id 404 → 'Page unavailable' + back link.
Reduced motion: toolbar/canvas transitions and any skeleton shimmer respect prefers-reduced-motion.

accessibility.contrastMin: 4.5:1 body text / 3:1 large text, UI components & focus ring (admin tone verified in token spec: text 16.9:1, muted 6.6:1, focus 3.9-4.6:1)
accessibility.focusOrder: logical DOM order — back link → PageHeader → title → slug → toolbar (single tab stop) → canvas → action bar → version history; visible :focus-visible 3px var(--lms-focus) ring offset 2px on every interactive element
accessibility.richTextEditor: canvas is role=textbox aria-multiline=true with accessible name 'Page content'; placeholder guidance via aria-describedby (not fake content); pasted/typed HTML is sanitized to an allow-list (security-agent DoD gate, architect D3)
accessibility.toolbar: WAI-ARIA Toolbar pattern: role=toolbar, single tab stop, Left/Right/Home/End arrow navigation, Enter/Space activate, Escape returns to canvas, aria-pressed on toggles, aria-controls→canvas, each control has a text name
accessibility.mediaEmbed: image embeds REQUIRE alt text (enforced) so authored content stays accessible; dialog is aria-modal with focus trap + Escape + focus restoration
accessibility.status: save status in an aria-live=polite region; errors via role=alert; lifecycle conveyed by Chip TEXT + tone (never color alone)
accessibility.motion: respect prefers-reduced-motion — disable transforms/shimmer, keep instant color state changes and static focus ring
accessibility.semantics: AppShell header/main landmarks; version history is an <aside>/<details>; list of versions is an <ol>; h1 page title → h2 section/aside headings; native controls labelled via Field; aria only where native won't do
accessibility.touchTargets: 44x44px minimum for all controls including toolbar icon buttons (36px icon + padding)
accessibility.noColorOnly: Draft/Published/Unsaved status always carries a text label in addition to tone

openQuestions: NET-NEW PATTERN: there is no rich-text editor in @lms/ui today. frontend-dev should choose a well-maintained accessible editor (e.g. TipTap/ProseMirror or a contenteditable wrapper) that supports the WAI-ARIA Toolbar pattern and produces clean HTML; visual chrome (toolbar, canvas) must use the --lms-* admin tokens above, NOT the library's default theme. Confirm whether to also add a reusable .lms-editor to @lms/ui or keep it admin-local for now (recommend admin-local for #32, promote later).

HTML SANITIZATION (security-agent DoD gate, architect D3/D4): body is stored as raw HTML server-side and NOT sanitized in the backend. The editor must sanitize on input AND the rendering path must sanitize on output. Confirm the allow-list (tags: p,h2,h3,h4,ul,ol,li,a,strong,em,u,img,video,blockquote,br; attrs: href,src,alt,title; strip script/style/event handlers).

AUTH ROLE: courses screens gate on isAdmin(session). Story #32 says 'As a teacher'. Confirm the role check for page authoring — does an instructor/teacher role pass the admin-app gate, or is authoring admin-only for now? Copy assumes 'teaching or administrator role'. Needs product confirmation (affects the permission-denied message + gate).

AUTO-SAVE vs MANUAL: design specifies explicit Save draft (matches D5 PATCH = new version on body change). Confirm we do NOT auto-save (auto-save would spawn a new immutable page_version on every keystroke/interval — version bloat, architect flagged unbounded growth). Recommend manual Save draft for #32.
PUBLISH CONFIRMATION: should Publish show a confirm step ('makes this page visible to learners')? Recommended yes for a destructive-ish/visibility action, but adds a modal. Confirm with product.

RESTORE a version: architect noted restore (POST .../restore) as a deferred follow-up, NOT in #32 DoD. This design provides View-only of historical versions. Confirm restore stays out of scope for #32.

createdBy is null from the backend (service-builder note: header resolver carries no user id) — the version history 'author' column may show 'Unknown' until user identity is threaded. Confirm acceptable for #32 or whether author display is required (would need a backend follow-up).
